DASHBOARD: DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP

PermID4296380340Websitehttp://www.davispolk.com/IndustryFull-Service BigLawAddress450 Lexington Avenue New York, NY 10017 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Davis Polk & Wardwell LLP, founded in 1849, is a global law firm headquartered in New York City. With over 1,000 lawyers across 10 offices, it serves clients in finance, technology, and healthcare, specializing in M&A, capital markets, and regulatory matters. In 2020, it advised on Morgan Stanleys $13 billion E*Trade acquisition and Gilead Sciences $21 billion Immunomedics deal. In 2024, it expanded its Menlo Park office with key hires like Paul Scrivano from Ropes & Gray. Generating over $1.5 billion annually, its culture emphasizes collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with Cravath, Davis Polk stands out for its dealmaking prowess, ranking second in 2020 M&A league tables. Recent initiatives include AI-driven legal tech and ESG advisory services.

+ DAVIS POLK & WARDWELL LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SIGENERGY'S HK IPO GAINS MOMENTUM WITH DAVIS POLK'S LEGAL GUIDANCE

Sigenergy Technology, a Chinese energy storage systems maker, has completed its Hong Kong IPO, raising HK$4.4 billion (about $562 million). The deal was guided by Davis Polk & Wardwell and Jia Yuan Law Offices, which provided crucial legal counsel throughout the process. The listing was recorded on the 17th of April 2026. Founded in May 2022 and based in Shanghai, Sigenergy specializes in distributed energy storage systems and develops stackable, solar-plus-storage all-in-one solutions. The company holds a 28.6 percent share of the global market for stackable, all-in-one DESS products, underscoring its leadership in a fast-growing sector. The offering was underwritten by a consortium including CITIC Securities and BNP Paribas, with CICC acting as joint sponsor. Legal support for the underwriters came from Linklaters and Jingtian & Gongcheng, with Linklaters’ Christine Xu, Lipton Li, and Queenie Tong advising. In related news, Mindspace Business Parks REIT’s Chennai IT Park acquisition for 30 billion rupees was advised by Khaitan & Co, while SBI Funds Management is preparing for a 130-billion-rupee IPO with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas and Linklaters advising. The Sigenergy IPO highlights rising investor interest in renewables and energy storage.

+ DAVIS POLK EXPANDS WEST COAST FOOTPRINT WITH LOS ANGELES OFFICE OPENING

Davis Polk & Wardwell said on 14th April 2026 that it will establish a Los Angeles office, appointing Jason Russell to lead the expansion. The move underscores the firm's bid to grow its West Coast footprint.Russell, a veteran litigator who previously ran Skadden Arps Slate Meagher & Flom's Los Angeles office and served on its policy committee, brings a portfolio of high-profile clients including the NBA, Christie’s and MGA Entertainment that the firm hopes to leverage in California.Davis Polk plans to hire additional lawyers for the new LA office and to accelerate its local capabilities, signaling an intent to compete with other leading California firms and win significant matters as the market heats up.

+ LIGHTYEAR CAPITAL, BACKED BY DAVIS POLK, NETS $2.5 BILLION FOR SIXTH FUND

Lightyear Capital, a private equity firm co-founded by Davis Polk & Wardwell, has raised $2.5 billion for its sixth fund, a milestone that underscores investor confidence in its strategy. The announcement came on April 14, 2026.The fund will target investments in financial services and technology, sectors that the firm sees as resilient and growth-focused. Davis Polk's legal services experience for financial institutions complements Lightyear's effort to identify opportunities and execute deals, reinforcing the collaboration between the two entities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ DEBEVOISE & PLIMPTON LONDON OFFICE REPORTS RECORD REVENUE OF $268 MILLION, UP 40%

Debevoise & Plimpton has reported a remarkable revenue surge of 40% in its London office, reaching a record $268 million. The results, announced on 7th April 2026, underscore the firm's strength in a competitive market.The growth is driven by higher demand for corporate law and dispute resolution services, with the firm's strategy of pursuing high-value client engagements and its ability to adapt to changing market conditions cited as key factors.Analysts expect the momentum to continue as Debevoise plans to invest in talent and broaden its service offerings, a strategy that could attract more clients and sustain revenue gains in the years ahead.

+ DEBEVOISE SECURES SECOND CIRCUIT AFFIRMATION FOR YPF IN $16 BILLION EXPROPRIATION CASE

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP secured an affirmance from the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Second Circuit on behalf of YPF S.A., the largest energy producer in Argentina. In a decision made on 30th March 2026, the court dismissed claims for more than $16 billion in damages and interest, ending a dispute that has lingered in the Southern District ofNew York since 2015. The ruling confirms the district court's assessment that YPF bore no liability for the asserted obligations.

+ DEBEVOISE ELEVATES SULLIVAN KC AND POPOVA TO EUROPEAN DISPUTES CO-CHAIRS

Debevoise & Plimpton LLP has appointed Jeffrey Sullivan KC and Ina Popova as Co-Chairs of European Disputes, effective immediately, expanding the firm's footprint in Paris, Frankfurt and Luxembourg. The appointment was announced on the 17th of March, 2026, underscoring Debevoise's commitment to strengthening international dispute resolution across mainland Europe.Catherine Amirfar, Co-Chair of Debevoise's global International Dispute Resolution Group, noted that Sullivan and Popova will build on the firm's long-standing strength in major disputes across mainland Europe and will continue delivering the highest level of advocacy and strategic counsel for clients. Sullivan KC is widely recognized for his work in international arbitration and public international law, focusing on high-stakes commercial and investor-state arbitrations; Popova brings broad international arbitration experience, dual civil and common-law qualifications, fluency in several languages, and previously served on the ICCInternational Court of Arbitration. Debevoise's International Dispute Resolution Group has more than four decades of experience and has handled matters across over 125 countries, with stakes exceeding $110 billion.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL [2 News That Matters]

+ RODGE COHEN REFLECTS ON FIVE DECADES SHAPING BANKING AT SULLIVAN & CROMWELL

H. Rodgin "Rodge" Cohen, the senior chair at Sullivan & Cromwell, has been a transformative force in banking for more than five decades. On 19th February 2026, at an NYU Law Institute for Corporate Governance & Finance (ICGF) event, Cohen spoke with candor about the arc of the modern financial system, the discussion joined by Jay Clayton, former chair of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, and Edward Rock, ICGF co-director.Cohen traced regulatory evolution from the postwar era to the present, emphasizing how landmark measures reshaped access to banking. He and Clayton highlighted the Bank Secrecy Act and the Community Reinvestment Act as turning points that broadened inclusion and redefined compliance burdens. Clayton noted the dramatic shifts since the 1970s, while Cohen underscored how these changes altered banks’ operations and risk profiles.Beyond regulation, Cohen’s influence extended to crisis work. He recalled the 2008 financial crisis, representing several major banks as policymakers moved to stabilize the system. Citing the hours before the government’s intervention following Lehman Brothers’ collapse, Cohen said, "We were on the edge of a cliff," a moment that underscored the urgency of decisive action and cemented his reputation as a leading financial-services lawyer. He also highlighted interstate banking moves in the 1980s that helped banks operate across state lines, reshaping the landscape. Clayton praised Cohen’s ability to interpret statutes creatively, a skill essential for navigating modern regulation.Looking ahead, Cohen warned that cryptocurrency regulation must stay in step with rapid market change. He cautioned that regulators risk creating weak rules if they lag behind innovation, invoking Gresham’s Law to argue that "Bad regulation drives out good regulation." The conversation underscored the ongoing evolution of the financial sector and reaffirmed the central role of firms like Sullivan & Cromwell in shaping its future.

+ SULLIVAN & CROMWELL ELEVATES LONDON FINANCE PRACTICE WITH TWO NEW PARTNERS

Sullivan & Cromwell LLP announced the appointment of Christopher McLaughlin and Alastair McVeigh as partners in its London office, expanding the firm’s private capital capabilities across the UK and Europe. The hires, disclosed in a Jan. 21, 2026 release, align with the firm’s ongoing UK and European expansion plans. McLaughlin will lead private equity sponsor and borrower acquisitions as well as leveraged finance in London, while McVeigh will head the European private credit offering with a focus on direct and specialty lending.Robert Giuffra and Scott Miller, Co-Chairs of Sullivan & Cromwell, welcomed the new partners and highlighted how their expertise will strengthen the firm’s ability to serve private equity sponsors, lenders, and private credit providers throughout the region.McLaughlin is recognized as a leading voice in acquisition and leveraged finance law in Europe, with extensive experience advising private equity sponsors and private credit providers. He has been ranked as a Leading Individual for Leveraged Finance by Chambers UK and as a Leading Partner for Acquisition Finance by Legal 500. McVeigh is likewise respected for his work in leveraged finance, particularly in private credit and specialty lending, making him a strong addition to the firm’s growing practice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB [3 News That Matters]

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB EXPANDS DIGITAL ASSETS TEAM WITH IGOR KLEYMAN RETURN

Cleary Gottlieb announced the reappointment of Igor Kleyman as a partner in its financial institutions practice, expanding the firm's focus on digital assets, artificial intelligence, and fintech. The firm disclosed the move on 23rd April 2026.Kleyman returns after nearly a decade advising clients on digital assets and AI. Most recently, he led Amazon's Responsible AI Governance, and previously held key roles at Nasdaq and Gemini Trust Company, where he navigated regulatory challenges and contributed to the development of pioneering financial products.Managing Partner Jeff Karpf expressed enthusiasm about Igor's return, stating, "Igor's fluency across the transformational fintech and AI sectors, combined with his sophisticated understanding of financial regulatory structures and institutions, enhances our powerhouse of innovation in financial services." This sentiment underscores the firm's strategy to remain at the forefront of legal services in these dynamic fields. Igor Kleyman added, "I look forward to collaborating across Cleary's global network to further strengthen our relationships with fintech companies and businesses exploring digital assets, tokenization, and AI offerings."

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB REINSTATES DIGITAL ASSETS AND AI SPECIALIST FROM AMAZON

Cleary Gottlieb announced the return of a digital assets and AI specialist who previously held a senior role at Amazon. The hire signals the firm's ongoing effort to deepen its capabilities in the fast-changing digital economy, where AI and digital asset management are increasingly intertwined. The professional's prior work at Amazon included leadership on AI initiatives and digital-asset programs, according to people familiar with the matter.On 20th April 2026, the firm confirmed that the specialist has rejoined after a successful tenure at Amazon. The move comes as legal frameworks governing digital currencies and AI applications become more complex, creating a need for experienced guidance. Cleary Gottlieb says the added expertise will strengthen its advisory work for clients in fintech, regulatory compliance, and related fields.

+ CLEARY GOTTLIEB UNVEILS CX+, AN AI PLATFORM TO OPTIMIZE LEGAL WORKFLOWS

Cleary Gottlieb has announced the launch of CX+, a proprietary AI-powered platform designed to enhance legal workflows through advanced contract intelligence and due diligence capabilities. The announcement was made on the 15th of April 2026, and CX+ is comprised of two main products: CX+Insights and CX+Transact, which cater specifically to the operational and transactional needs of in-house legal teams and deal professionals.Developed in collaboration with senior in-house clients and legal operations experts, CX+ was crafted to address the challenges faced by legal teams. Carla Swansburg, CEO of ClearyX, said, "We spent years speaking with clients about the problems that they wish technology would solve for them, and that generic tools simply aren't solving." The platform is designed to be adaptable and model-agnostic, allowing clients to configure it to their workflows, with emphasis on security and API integrations that enable immediate value from the outset.CX+ is split into two offerings: CX+Insights, which pulls in contracts to deliver AI-driven questions and dashboards, and CX+Transact, which supports contract review and M&A due diligence with tagging, filters and in-place editing. ClearyX also notes that customers can license CX+ as a standalone tool or alongside ClearyX services, and that the platform has demonstrated effective time and cost savings on diligence tasks across more than 150 deals, highlighting the platform's potential to streamline client service.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ LATHAM & WATKINS LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ LATHAM & WATKINS AND LOEB & LOEB LEAD $250 MILLION SPAC DEAL FOR AI BATTERY COMPANY

Two leading law firms, Latham & Watkins and Loeb & Loeb, are guiding the $250 million merger of AI Battery Company with a SPAC. The deal is designed to bolster the battery company's finances and speed its growth in the market for AI-driven energy storage solutions. The merger is expected to close shortly after regulatory approvals.The deal, announced on 21st April 2026, positions AI Battery Company to push ahead with AI-enabled battery technology, aiming to improve efficiency and sustainability while expanding manufacturing capabilities. The transaction provides capital for intensified R&D and scaling production.Latham & Watkins brings expertise in SPAC transactions and capital markets, while Loeb & Loeb contributes regulatory compliance and corporate governance guidance. Investors are watching the process as demand for AI-integrated solutions remains strong, and the SPAC route offers a clearer path to public ownership. The parties are optimistic about a smooth close and continued growth in the AI battery space.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS REPORTS RECORD GLOBAL REVENUE OF $8.3 BILLION FOR FY2025

Latham & Watkins LLP reported record results for fiscal year 2025, with global gross revenue of $8.3 billion, an 18.6% rise from about $7 billion in 2024. The results were announced on April 16, 2026, alongside the firm’s submissions for the 2026 Am Law 100 rankings released on the same day.Profit per equity partner rose to about $8.7 million, up 21.3% from roughly $7.1 million in 2024, underscoring the firm’s robust profitability.A key highlight is Latham’s London office, which crossed the $1 billion revenue threshold for the first time, up from about $850 million in 2024, signifying growth of more than 17%. The London office now employs around 1,000 staff and remains the firm’s largest non-U.S. location as it executes its Europe-focused growth strategy.The firm benefited from strong demand in core practices, including mergers and acquisitions, private equity, finance, and capital markets. Latham led nearly 800 M&A deals in 2025 with combined values exceeding $720 billion, and revenue per lawyer is estimated at about $2.23 million for 2025, based on a headcount of roughly 3,700 lawyers. These results come amid Am Law 100 trends showing broader revenue growth and improving profitability across leading firms.

+ LATHAM & WATKINS REPORTS RECORD $8.3 BILLION REVENUE, ELEVATING ITS GLOBAL STANDING

Latham & Watkins on 15th April 2026 reported global revenue of $8.3 billion, marking a new firm record and reinforcing its status as one of the world’s most influential law firms.The surge reflects broad demand across core practices, with clients seeking complex litigation, regulatory counsel, and large-scale transactions. Latham’s ability to scale across borders while sustaining profitability signals a market environment that rewards high-value work and sophisticated advisory capabilities.Revenue gains came notably from transactional disciplines such as capital markets and private equity, along with stronger activity in restructuring and finance. The firm’s diversified, leverage-driven model and global footprint have supported steady growth, and leadership suggests continued hiring and competitive compensation to attract top talent as competition for top lawyers intensifies.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SKADDEN, ARPS, SLATE, MEAGHER & FLOM, L.L.P. [3 News That Matters]

+ SKADDEN PROMOTES NINE LONDON PARTNERS AS NON-EQUITY TIER DEBUTS GLOBALLY

Skadden has promoted nine London partners as part of a 55-strong global round, the firm's first since it introduced a non-equity partner tier. The step reflects a broader move among US firms to refine compensation structures and retain top talent.This year's cohort more than doubles last year's 22, with London's intake rising markedly from the City's historically modest rounds.The nine new London partners are Jonathan Benson in White Collar Defense and Investigations, Zoe Q. Cooper Sutton in Finance, Benjamin T. Davies in Corporate, Kara L. Heggs in Tax, Jason Hewitt and Nicola Kerr-Shaw in National Security, Feargal Ryan in Financial Institutions, Kenneth J. Ryan in Capital Markets, and Cristina Tomassini in Corporate.

+ SKADDEN REPRESENTS OTSUKA IN USD 1.2 BILLION ACQUISITION OF TRANSCEND THERAPEUTICS

Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom is advising Tokyo-based Otsuka Pharmaceutical on its acquisition of clinical-stage biotechnology company Transcend Therapeutics in a deal potentially valued at USD1.2 billion. As of 1st April 2026, the transaction is set to bolster Otsuka's portfolio in the competitive biotech landscape.Under the terms of the agreement, Otsuka will pay USD700 million to Transcend shareholders upon completion, anticipated in the second quarter of 2026. In addition, Otsuka could pay up to USD525 million in contingent consideration based on future sales milestones tied to assets currently in development.Transcend is being advised by New York-based Kirkland & Ellis, led by partners Graham Robinson and Laura Knoll. They are supported by Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman partner Ron Fleming Jr. The financial advisory for Transcend has been handled by Centerview Partners, with partners Andrew S Rymer and Jason Zuckerbrod, alongside principal Tommaso Verderame. The Skadden engagement underscores the growing pace of biotech mergers and acquisitions as companies seek to bolster capabilities amid regulatory complexities and evolving market dynamics.

+ ALICORP EXPANDS LATIN AMERICAN FOOTPRINT AS SIMPSON THACHER AND SKADDEN ADVISE ON FLORA FOOD GROUP DEAL

Simpson Thacher and Skadden have advised Alicorp on the acquisition of Flora Food Group's brand portfolio, a move that will widen Alicorp's reach across 17 Latin American markets, excluding Mexico and Brazil, and secure a manufacturing facility in Cali, Colombia. The deal was announced on 18th March 2026.The transaction is part of Alicorp's broader aim to diversify its offerings and strengthen its competitive position by adding established brands and operational capabilities. The involvement of Simpson Thacher and Skadden underscores the cross-border nature of the deal and the importance of experienced M&A counsel for Alicorp's regional growth.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS [3 News That Matters]

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS HIRES PRIVATE CAPITAL SPECIALIST WILL AITKEN-DAVIES TO STRENGTHEN LONDON PRACTICE

Kirkland & Ellis has announced the addition of Will Aitken-Davies as a corporate partner in its London office, expanding its private capital practice. The hire is effective immediately and aims to strengthen capabilities in private equity and infrastructure investor transactions across European and cross-border markets. The announcement was made on 29th April 2026 at 16:34:10.Aitken-Davies joins from Paul Weiss, bringing experience in complex acquisitions, carve-outs, co-investments, public M&A, consortium deals, and hybrid equity investments. His sector coverage includes energy and infrastructure, industrials, technology, and financial services; he has been ranked in Chambers UK for Private Equity Buyouts, as well as in Legal 500 UK and IFLR1000.Jon A. Ballis, Chairman of the Executive Committee at Kirkland & Ellis, described Will as a trusted adviser to leading financial investors on sophisticated, cross-border European transactions, and said his arrival strengthens the firm’s market-leading team’s ability to support sponsors executing at scale across London and Europe. Executive Committee Member Matthew Elliott added that Aitken-Davies is a market-leading dealmaker whose European M&A experience will enhance the firm’s integrated advisory capabilities.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS EXPANDS HONG KONG LITIGATION PRACTICE WITH HIRE OF NEW PARTNER

Kirkland & Ellis has announced the appointment of Wilfred Ho as a partner in its Hong Kong litigation practice, a move designed to strengthen the firm’s capabilities across the Asia-Pacific region. The announcement was made on 29th April 2026, underscoring the firm's commitment to delivering enhanced cross-border dispute resolution services.Ho joins from White & Case in Hong Kong, where he was a partner focusing on international arbitration and commercial litigation. With more than a decade of experience advising Asian corporates and private equity funds on cross-border disputes across Greater China, he previously honed his practice at Skadden. Ho's work covers shareholder disputes, insolvency and restructuring, and intellectual property, serving clients in gaming, media, entertainment, hospitality, technology and energy. In arbitration, he has represented clients under HKIAC, CIETAC, ICC, NAI and SIAC rules. Neil McDonald, a Kirkland & Ellis partner, described Ho as a transformative addition to the Asia litigation team.

+ KIRKLAND & ELLIS EXPANDS PHILADELPHIA FOOTPRINT WITH ADAM PRINCE APPOINTMENT

Kirkland & Ellis announced the hire of Adam Prince as a partner in its Corporate Practice Group in Philadelphia, effective 27th April 2026. Prince will bolster the firm's transactional capabilities, with a focus on private equity, mergers and acquisitions, investments, recapitalizations and other complex transactions, particularly in the healthcare sector.Prince previously was a Morgan Lewis partner, advising private equity sponsors, portfolio companies and growth-stage businesses on transactions ranging from early-stage investments to multibillion-dollar deals and serving as outside general counsel to growth-stage companies.The Philadelphia office, launched in 2025, has grown to about 20 lawyers across litigation and transactional practices. Jon A. Ballis, Chairman of the Executive Committee, welcomed Prince, saying, "Adam is a terrific lawyer with a stellar reputation both in private equity and in the Philadelphia community, and he is the perfect fit to help build our transactional practice as we grow in the city." Leo Greenberg, a Corporate Partner, noted the benefits of adding Prince alongside Stephanie Haas as the firm expands its Philadelphia presence. Prince himself said, "Kirkland is no doubt the premier law firm for sophisticated private equity work. Combining this platform with the chance to build a top team here in Philadelphia with Steph and all of the welcoming, energetic and collaborative partners I’ve met so far, I couldn’t be more excited to take on this opportunity."

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT [3 News That Matters]

+ KKR TO TAKE TAIYO HOLDINGS PRIVATE IN $3.3 BILLION TENDER OFFER BACKED BY MAJOR STAKEHOLDERS

KKR has announced plans to acquire all outstanding shares of Taiyo Holdings for 528.56 billion yen, about $3.3 billion, through a tender offer. The bid is supported by key shareholders and is being advised by Simpson Thacher & Bartlett. The announcement was made on 10th April 2026.The offer price of 4,750 yen per share represents a 4.7% discount to Taiyo's last closing price. Backers including DIC Corp, Kowa Co and Oasis Management together hold 42.2% of Taiyo's outstanding shares and have signaled strong support for KKR's privatization plan.Simpson Thacher & Bartlett is advising KKR, with a team led by partners Noritaka Kumamoto and Jonathan Stradling, along with Daisuke Ueta and Kaoruko Sakuramoto on mergers and acquisitions. Antitrust matters are being handled by partner Etienne Renaudeau and associates Dani Rabinowitz, Luke Cowdell and Giovanna De Cristofaro, while credit-related issues are led by partner Makiko Harunari, counsel Atsushi Usui and associate Mark Watson.

+ SIMPSON THACHER GUIDES NSSK TO RECORD NSSK IV FUND FORMATION

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett advised the Japanese private equity firm Nippon Sangyo Suishin Kiko (NSSK) on forming the NSSK IV Series Funds, securing total commitments of JPY250 billion (USD1.5 billion). The milestone was reached on 10th April 2026, with the offering closing on 31st March 2026.The global team provided comprehensive counsel, focusing on regulatory and compliance matters essential to the fund's formation. Leading partners included David Azcue, co-head of the private funds practice in Japan; Adam Furber, co-head of the Asia offices and head of the Asia investment funds practice; and Makiko Harunari, head of the Asia banking and credit practice.The NSSK IV Series Funds were more than twice oversubscribed, attracting commitments from over 100 institutional investors worldwide. This level of demand underscores strong investor confidence in NSSK's strategy and signals growing interest in private equity in the Asia-Pacific region.

+ SIMPSON THACHER EXPANDS CAPITAL STRUCTURE SOLUTIONS WITH NEW PARTNER ADDITIONS

Simpson Thacher & Bartlett announced the addition of partners Christine Bae and Jacob Ruby to its Capital Structure Solutions Practice, reinforcing the firm's commitment to complex financing and restructuring matters as the practice expands after its early-2026 launch. The announcement, dated 18th March 2026, underscores the firm's plan to broaden advisory capabilities in liability management, private credit, and special situations under the leadership of David Nemecek.Bae and Ruby bring extensive experience advising sponsors, companies, lenders, and investors on intricate capital-structure challenges. The Capital Structure Solutions Practice now integrates the firm’s teams in liability management, special situations, and restructuring with its broader financing capabilities to provide coordinated guidance across financing strategy, credit solutions, distressed M&A, and Chapter 11 restructurings. David Nemecek stated, "Christine and Jacob bring deep technical capability, sound judgment, and the ability to craft bespoke solutions for clients navigating their most complex capital structure challenges." Both hires also emphasized Simpson Thacher's collaborative culture and integrated platform as key factors supporting their practice.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WACHTELL, LIPTON, ROSEN & KATZ LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ SYSCO HIRES JONES DAY AND WACHTELL FOR $29 BILLION JETRO ACQUISITION

Sysco Corporation has engaged Jones Day for antitrust counsel as it moves to acquire rival Jetro Restaurant Depot for a $29 billion deal. The law firm Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP will advise on the merger, and the appointment was confirmed on 31st March 2026.The transaction is intended to bolster Sysco’s leadership in the competitive food distribution market, which has faced rising costs and supply chain pressures. With Jetro’s established network in the restaurant-supply sector, Sysco aims to enhance operations and broaden its customer base, while industry observers expect a thorough regulatory review given the deal’s size.Jones Day’s antitrust practice is well-regarded for guiding major deals, and Wachtell’s experience in complex mergers will be central to navigating the legal landscape of this transaction. The collaboration underscores the importance of seasoned counsel in large-scale consolidations and is likely to influence how regulators approach similar deals in the sector.

+ WACHTELL LIPTON GUIDES PUBLIC STORAGE IN $10.5 BILLION ALL-STOCK ACQUISITION OF NATIONAL STORAGE AFFILIATES

Public Storage announced on 24th March 2026 that it will acquire National Storage Affiliates in a $10.5 billion all-stock transaction. Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz LLP is advising Public Storage, the largest self-storage company in the United States by market capitalization, and Clifford Chance is representing National Storage Affiliates in this all-stock deal.Under the agreement, National Storage shareholders will receive 0.14 of a Public Storage common share for each National Storage share, valuing the consideration at $41.68 per share. The transaction is expected to close in the third quarter of 2026, after the formation of a joint venture that will encompass 313 properties with an estimated value of about $3.3 billion, expanding Public Storage's footprint in key markets. Real estate financing counsel to Public Storage is provided by DLA Piper, led by Amy Carbins and supported by Viktor Sapezhnikov.

+ X CORP DROPS $90 MILLION SUIT AGAINST WACHTELL LIPTON, ENDS LEGAL BATTLE

In a swift legal pivot, Elon Musk’s X Corp has voluntarily dismissed its high-stakes $90 million lawsuit against powerhouse law firm Wachtell Lipton Rosen & Katz, closing a bitter chapter from the chaotic 2022 Twitter takeover. The lawsuit, filed in San FranciscoSuperior Court back on 1st October 2023, accused Wachtell of pocketing an 'improper bonus payment' that violated fiduciary duties, claiming Twitter executives inflated fees with 'success' bonuses just before the $44 billion deal closed.Wachtell Lipton, which represented the old Twitter board in the fierce battle to compel Musk to honor his buyout pledge, responded to the accusations with vigor. The firm emphasized its 'complete success' after months of litigation, which ultimately secured billions for shareholders. They asserted that the fees in question were approved by the board itself, effectively countering the claims made by X Corp.The dismissal of the lawsuit, which was revealed in a court filing reported by Reuters, bars refiling and indicates a clean break between the two parties. A spokesperson for Wachtell Lipton expressed satisfaction with the outcome, stating, 'We are pleased that X has dismissed its meritless lawsuit with prejudice. There was no settlement.' This marks a significant turn in the ongoing legal saga surrounding the Twitter acquisition.This legal retreat comes on the heels of another reconciliation for X Corp, which recently acknowledged a $128 million executive payout suit related to the tumultuous period during the Twitter acquisition. The dismissal signals a potential shift in strategy for X Corp, as it seeks to distance itself from the financial and legal entanglements of its predecessor.As the legal landscape continues to evolve for Elon Musk's X Corp, the resolution with Wachtell Lipton may pave the way for a more focused approach to its business operations. Investors and market observers will be keenly watching how these developments influence the company's trajectory moving forward.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CRAVATH, SWAINE & MOORE LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ CRAVATH SWAINE & MOORE TO LEAD £800 MILLION WATER SUSTAINABILITY FUNDRAISING

On 30th of April 2026, Cravath Swaine & Moore announced it will spearhead a £800 million fundraising campaign aimed at bolstering water sustainability initiatives. The plan is designed to attract a broad base of institutional and private investors seeking exposure to sustainable water management solutions.The initiative will focus on innovative technologies and infrastructure improvements that boost water efficiency and reduce waste. Cravath Swaine & Moore will help structure the financial frameworks needed for such a large-scale investment, with Slaughter and May expected to collaborate.By linking capital with strategic partnerships in the water sector, the campaign aims to set a benchmark for future investments and to bolster investor confidence amid rising attention to environmental sustainability.

+ CRAVATH PARTNER LAUNCHES BOUTIQUE DEDICATED TO CORPORATE INVESTIGATIONS

A Cravath Swaine & Moore partner has launched a boutique law firm dedicated to corporate investigations, signaling a shift toward specialized counsel as companies navigate increasingly complex regulatory demands. The new practice will offer internal investigations, compliance assessments, and risk management guidance, leveraging the founder’s Cravath experience to tailor services to clients under scrutiny.On 9th March 2026, the launch underscored a wider trend of seasoned practitioners leaving traditional firm structures to build agile, focused practices. The boutique aims to serve clients facing intricate corporate reviews with a high-touch, confidentiality-forward approach that larger firms often struggle to match.Observers anticipate a diverse roster, including Fortune 500 companies, private equity firms, and startups. The founder’s network and Cravath pedigree are expected to lend immediate credibility, while the emphasis on discretion and bespoke handling aims to differentiate the firm in a crowded market.

+ BIOCON SUCCEEDS $1.2 BILLION BIOSIMILARS DEAL, ENGAGING MULTIPLE LAW FIRMS

Biocon, the Indian biopharmaceutical company, has closed a $1.2 billion deal to fully consolidate its biosimilars operations by acquiring the remaining stakes from minority partners. The transaction, disclosed on December 11, 2025, is designed to streamline Biocon's footprint in a fast expanding biosimilars market and strengthen its growth trajectory.Viatris agreed to divest its remaining stake for about $815 million as part of the package, while other sellers include True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II. Serum Institute Life Sciences was among the sellers, with Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advising it. Khaitan & Co and Cravath, Swaine & Moore advised Viatris on financing matters, and Veritas Legal provided counsel to True North and Tata Capital Growth Fund II.Biocon tapped Shardul Amarchand Mangaldas to lead its advisory team, with Raghubir Menon and Natashaa Shroff at the helm. The firm also included Tarumoy Chaudhuri and Kriti Saraswat. The deal aligns with Biocon's ongoing integration with Viatris and its debt refinancing plans, underscoring the company's push to consolidate its biosimilars business amid a competitive market.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MILBANK LLP [3 News That Matters]

+ MILBANK MEMORIAL FUND ANNOUNCES OPEN RECRUITMENT FOR LEADERSHIP DEVELOPMENT COHORTS

On the 1st of May, 2026, the Milbank Memorial Fund announced that applications are now open for the 2026-2027 cohorts of its Milbank Fellows Program (MFP) and Emerging Leaders Program (ELP), both designed to bolster government health policy leadership. The application window will run through June 19th, 2026. The MFP targets senior leaders in state governments, while the ELP is aimed at rising leaders in state, large-county, and large-city governments. Participants will have all associated costs covered and will collaborate with peers from across regions. The curriculum emphasizes leadership development, understanding complex health policy topics, and strengthening the ability to implement effective health policies in communities. Selection will emphasize a commitment to health improvement and addressing health disparities, with candidates holding influential roles in the legislative or executive branches and a willingness to learn and share expertise on population health policy. The Milbank Memorial Fund invites government officials to apply or nominate exceptional individuals who meet these qualifications.

+ MILBANK TRAINEE BREAKS WORLD RECORD IN LONDON MARATHON COSTUME

Archie Hunt, a trainee solicitor at Milbank, set a new Guinness World Record by finishing the London Marathon in a Captain Underpants costume in 2 hours 27 minutes on Apr. 28, 2026. His time eclipsed the previous record of 2:42 for the fastest marathon dressed as a book character.Balancing a demanding legal role with months of rigorous training, Hunt ran early-morning sessions of more than 20 kilometers while donning the full costume. The attempt followed Guinness World Records guidelines, with official timing, witness statements, and photographic evidence ensuring the record was verifiable.The run was also a fundraiser for Evelina London Children’s Charity, supporting the hospital where Hunt receives treatment for dilated cardiomyopathy. He finished in the top 0.1% of participants at a World Major marathon, and on finishing he said the record achievement is a sign of what can be accomplished despite health challenges. He hopes to inspire others with DCM and eyes a future sub-2:20 marathon, with a June ultra in Finland already on his calendar.

+ MILBANK UNVEILS 12-YEAR PLAN TO SHIELD ASH TREES FROM EMERALD ASH BORER

On Apr. 21, 2026, Milbank city administrator Steve Pendergrass outlined the financial impact of an invasive emerald ash borer threat, noting that removing affected trees could reach up to $4 million. Milbank's canopy is heavily weighted toward ash - more than half of its trees are ash, including in parks and the cemetery.In response, the city is pursuing a 'herd immunity' strategy designed to fortify the ash canopy over the next 12 years at an estimated cost of about $2.4 million. The approach emphasizes targeted treatment and monitoring to save existing trees while reducing pest spread, a model the city hopes other South Dakota communities can adapt.Local officials, environmental experts, and residents are expected to collaborate as treatments are phased, focusing first on vulnerable areas. Milbank argues the plan balances preservation of green space with proactive pest management, aiming to create a resilient urban forest that can withstand invasive threats.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHR-KV33-S2T4-W23W-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY MONITOR: TRENDS AND PEER UPDATES

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT49-S561-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-SVN3-S56J-J251-00000-00OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:05:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKOCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP - INDUSTRY SPOTLIGHT: KEY TRENDS AND PEER HIGHLIGHTS

DASHBOARD: OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP

PermID5073147691Websitehttps://octopus.energy/IndustryRenewable Energy Supply & GenerationAddress"UK House, 5th floor, 164-182 Oxford Street London W1D 1NN England, United Kingdom"

ACTIVITIES:

Octopus Energy Group, founded in 2015 by Greg Jackson, is a renewable energy provider headquartered in London, UK. Employing over 3,500 people, it generates $5 billion annually, serving 7 million+ customers across 18 countries. Its Kraken platform optimizes energy usage. In 2024, Octopus expanded U.S. operations. Its culture emphasizes sustainability, innovation, and customer focus, with training in clean tech and AI. Competing with EDF, Octopus stands out for its flexible tariffs and smart grid tech. Recent initiatives include EV charging networks and net-zero commitments, aligning with green energy trends. Valued at $9 billion in 2023, Octopus drives the global energy transition.

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GROUP [3 Press Releases]

+ OCTOPUS ENERGY GENERATION TO INVEST $500 MILLION TO REMOVE POLLUTING CO₂ FROM THE ATMOSPHERE

Major US deal to help cut up to 50 million tonnes of CO₂ - equivalent to eliminating the annual greenhouse gas emissions of New York City

Carbon credits to be snapped up by Google, Meta and McKinsey

Pushes Octopus closer to its ambitious $2bn US investment target, with a significant proportion in California's clean tech scene

London / San Francisco, 30 April 2026 - Octopus Energy Generation, one of Europe's leading renewables investors, is ramping up efforts to slash CO₂ pollution at scale - inking a major US deal that will help remove up to 50 million tonnes of carbon dioxide from the air.

Octopus's fund management team is set to invest $500 million in afforestation and reforestation projects in the US developed by public benefit and climate technology company Living Carbon.

Source: Company Website

+ POWERED UP: OCTOPUS AND MUIRHOUSE SLASH ENERGY BILLS FOR SCOTTISH SOCIAL HOMES BY £200

Muirhouse becomes one of Scotland's first housing associations to roll out Octopus Energy's groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff

UK's first green tech energy tariff that rewards both social housing landlords and tenants

Slashes electricity bills for social housing tenants by around £200 a year

Helping green home upgrades deliver real returns for landlords

London/Edinburgh, 29th April 2026 - Octopus Energy, the UK's largest energy supplier, has partnered with Muirhouse Housing Association to bring its groundbreaking Tenant Power tariff to over 30 social homes in Scotland.

Source: Company Website

+ 1 MILLION CUSTOMERS FOR EVERY TENTACLE: OCTOPUS ENERGY SUPERCHARGES TO 8 MILLION CUSTOMERS IN THE UK

The UK's largest energy supplier announces hitting 8 million home energy customers, with 2500 new customers joining daily

Market leading support for customers now includes You Pay We Pay, OctoAssist fund and the extension of their electric blanket scheme for Winter 2026

The milestone coincides with a doubling in heat pump sales and a 0.75 rise in solar sales as households shift to trusting in home grown power

London, 24th April 2026 - Octopus Energy has surpassed 8 million customers in the UK, further cementing its position as the nation's largest energy supplier.

With 2500 customers switching to Octopus every day, the gap has widened since Octopus claimed the top spot in January 2025, the first time the market lead had changed hands since privatisation in 1986.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. [3 Press Releases]

+ ECOTALK REINTRODUCE BEAVERS TO EAST SUSSEX AFTER A 400-YEAR ABSENCE

We've got amazing news from Ecotalk this spring with the release of a family of (extremely cute!) beavers into Bowyers Wood, East Sussex.

Chris Packham, Ecotalk's Chief Ecologist, was there to help the beavers get settled in. He said: "I just can't remember when I was last so excited about meeting a rodent! Big, fantastic rodents! What a fantastic day for Bowyers. And massive thanks to all our Ecotalk customers for making this possible."

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD.: GOOD NEWS AND BAD NEWS: OUR BREAKING THE LINK CAMPAIGN IS WORKING - BUT WE NEED TO GO FURTHER

Britain's energy market is broken. For years, we've been campaigning to "break the link" between the soaring cost of fossil gas and the price of the cheap, green electricity we generate right here in Britain.

Now, the message is finally hitting home in the corridors of Westminster.

There was a flurry of excitement in mid-April 2026 as the chancellor, Rachel Reeves, announced that the government was looking at ways to break the link between electricity and fossil gas prices.

Source: Company Website

+ ECOTRICITY GROUP LTD. OUR DAY WITH THE YOUNG GREEN BRITON CHALLENGE

Four members of the Ecotricity team recently took the hot seats as the judges at Stroud High School for entries to the Young Green Briton Challenge: a national programme that transforms students' climate ideas into real-world action.

For the 'Eco Dragon's Den', our team drew on their real-world expertise in green energy and sustainability. They used this to guide and challenge students on the impact and feasibility of their projects, offering constructive feedback to help sharpen each idea.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Past Month: Monthly: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE:ED) falls 7% on thin volume

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE:ED $106.84), the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market cap, has fell $8.59 (or 7.4%) in the past month to close at $106.84 on Wednesday May 06. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 753.3 points (or 11.4%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -18.8%.

The volume was 0.7 times average trading of 50.1 million shares.

Activities

Consolidated Edison, Inc. (NYSE: ED) is a leading energy utility company headquartered in New York, serving over 3.5 million customers in New York City and Westchester County. Its core business activities encompass the transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity, natural gas, and steam, supporting residential, commercial, and industrial sectors with a focus on reliability and safety. The company operates through subsidiaries like Consolidated Edison Company of New York and Orange and Rockland Utilities, emphasizing infrastructure maintenance and regulatory compliance. Fastest-growing segments include renewable energy integration, such as solar and wind projects, as well as electric vehicle infrastructure and grid modernization initiatives, driven by rising demand for sustainable energy solutions and state-level clean energy mandates. These efforts have bolstered ConEd's revenue growth, positioning it as a key player in the transition to a low-carbon economy while maintaining operational excellence. It is the NYSE's 6th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

Past Month: Monthly: Dow Inc (NYSE:DOW) falls 7% on average volume

Dow Inc (NYSE:DOW $38.48), the NYSE's 2nd largest Basic Materials company by market cap, has fell $2.91 (or 7.0%) in the past month to close at $38.48 on Wednesday May 06, ending a five-month streak of rises. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which rose 753.3 points (or 11.4%) in the month, this represented a relative price change of -18.4%.

The volume was 0.9 times average trading of 270 million shares.

Activities

Dow Inc., a global leader in materials science and a constituent of the New York Stock Exchange, focuses on the production and innovation of performance materials, chemicals, and plastics.
